growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron's March 18 quarter reinforced that HBM and AI server DRAM are driving the memory upcycle, making Micron a direct beneficiary of the 2026 AI memory bottleneck rather than a generic PC-memory recovery story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron is the cleanest public way to own the 2026 AI memory bottleneck, with filing-backed evidence of record profitability, HBM4 volume shipments and a still-compressed valuation versus its earnings power.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Profitable mega-cap software compounder now sits 36.95% below its 52-week high and just 6.93% above its low despite 9.58% trailing sales growth and 17.96% profit margins; the why-now trigger is management commencing a $25 billion accelerated share repurchase on March 16, 2026 with the next earnings report estimated for May 27, 2026, though the bear case is that AI pressure and decelerating seat growth keep the multiple compressed.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce is a high-margin, cash-rich software compounder trading like a no-growth SaaS name even as Agentforce traction, aggressive buybacks, and insider buying point to a better setup than the market is pricing.

Atlassian looks mispriced after a 71.8% drawdown because Q2 FY26 still showed 23% revenue growth, 26% cloud growth, 44% RPO growth, and strong free cash flow, while the March restructuring is aimed at accelerating AI and enterprise execution rather than plugging a balance-sheet hole.

Alphabet remains a high-quality AI and cloud compounder with 18% revenue growth, 48% Google Cloud growth, and major spring product catalysts, while the current setup still offers attractive upside despite capex and antitrust risks.

Crocs' core brand remains a high-margin cash generator trading at about 6x forward earnings, and the stock can rerate if May results confirm HEYDUDE is stabilizing while buybacks keep shrinking the share count.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Constellation remains one of the cleanest public ways to express institutional rotation into AI-power and nuclear-backed electricity scarcity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Constellation pairs scarce nuclear generation with the newly acquired Calpine gas fleet, giving it unusually strong leverage to AI-driven power demand and long-term contracted growth, though the stock still trades at a premium multiple.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Delta stands out as a cash-generative operator with record revenue and strong free-cash-flow guidance whose shares have been hit by macro and travel fears more than by company-specific deterioration.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Delta combines sub-9x earnings valuation with strong free cash flow, and March demand trends improved enough for management to raise revenue guidance ahead of the April 8 earnings report despite fuel pressure.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Vistra fits the same electricity-scarcity theme but with a more diversified gas-plus-nuclear profile that can still benefit from hyperscaler load growth.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Contracted nuclear PPAs, higher 2026 cash-flow guidance, and new investment-grade ratings support upside, but leverage and execution risk keep Vistra below STRONG BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Sysco became a contrarian idea after the market punished its Restaurant Depot deal even though the transaction gives it immediate scale in a faster-growing cash-and-carry channel.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Sysco's post-deal selloff looks overdone versus a still-cash-generative core business, reaffirmed FY2026 guidance, and a strategically sensible expansion into higher-margin cash-and-carry, though the debt load keeps this from being high conviction.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Benzinga's options scanner flagged a bullish May 15, 2026 $120 put sweep in Citigroup totaling about $2.3M across 2,458 contracts, an unusually large premium outlay that implies institutions are leaning constructive and willing to own stock near the strike.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Citigroup is still inexpensive at roughly 1.0x book and 9.3x forward earnings, with April earnings, a May investor day, and ongoing capital return creating a credible rerating path if execution holds.

Synopsys is a high-quality EDA and engineering-software franchise trading well below its peak, with strong free cash flow and tangible Ansys integration catalysts, though China/export and execution risk keep this out of STRONG BUY territory.
Blackstone is a high-quality alternative asset manager with renewed realizations, strong fundraising and AI/data-center exposure, but the stock is not cheap enough to ignore liquidity and cycle risk.

Credo has real AI interconnect momentum with record fiscal Q3 results, strong June earnings catalyst, and expanding optical/AEC design wins, but concentration and execution risk keep this below top-tier conviction.

FDA approval of relacorilant in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer gives Corcept a new commercial growth leg, but generic Korlym risk and the unresolved hypercortisolism CRL keep the setup below strong-buy quality.

High-margin AI memory and interface-IP supplier is still compounding on record product revenue and fresh HBM4E/data-center catalysts, though the stock is not cheap and concentration risk remains material.

Mattel looks undervalued versus its cash flow and buyback capacity, and 2026 catalysts around Mattel163, entertainment releases, and the next earnings report can re-rate the stock if margin pressure stabilizes.

CNXC screens as a deep-value rerating candidate because management maintained FY2026 revenue and adjusted free-cash-flow guidance, refinancing reduced part of the near-term maturity overhang, and the CEO made an open-market purchase near current levels, but weak organic growth and leverage keep this out of high-conviction territory.

Microsoft remains a high-quality AI and cloud compounder with strong cash generation and a 33% drawdown from its 52-week high, but the stock still needs to prove that surging AI capex will convert into durable margin-accretive growth.

Cheap cyclically depressed gaming hardware name with positive FCF, a newly authorized $50 million buyback, and improving margins, but still capped by a cautious 2026 revenue outlook and tariff-sensitive supply chain.
De-rated semiconductor cash machine with record Q1 revenue, rising automotive and data-center exposure, and a fresh $20B buyback, though near-term handset and Apple modem headwinds cap upside.

AVLAYAH's March 2026 FDA approval de-risks Denali's blood-brain-barrier platform and creates a real launch story, but the tiny market, confirmatory-trial dependence, and ongoing cash burn keep conviction below high.
Aptiv looks mispriced after the Versigent separation, with a real April-May catalyst stack and cheap forward valuation, but cyclical auto and tariff exposure keep it out of strong-buy territory.

DPZ is a high-quality cash-generating franchised restaurant compounder with a near-term earnings catalyst, but the upside case is tempered by leverage and a still-competitive value-driven pizza market.

Alkami is a beaten-down vertical SaaS name with 34.7% Q4 revenue growth, $480.3 million of ARR up 35%, positive free cash flow, and unusually strong March insider accumulation ahead of its April 29 earnings report.

UiPath is a profitable, cash-rich automation platform trading well below its highs, and if agentic automation launches plus buybacks translate into steadier ARR growth, the stock looks undervalued at current levels.
Post-breach recovery and large insider buying make CPNG watchable, but unresolved regulatory fallout and a still-rich valuation on thin margins keep it out of buy territory.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Molina became interesting because multiple senior insiders stepped in with sizable open-market buys after a brutal reset, which is one of the cleaner confidence signals in the managed-care selloff.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap after a 63% drawdown, but Molina still needs to prove that Medicaid rate relief can outrun elevated utilization and margin pressure before the reset becomes investable.

catalysts_general: Discovery: United Therapeutics printed a fresh biotech catalyst with late-March TETON-1 success, adding meaningful upside optionality beyond its already cash-generative pulmonary franchise.
growth_picks.analyst_take: United Therapeutics has a high-quality, cash-generative pulmonary franchise and multiple newly de-risked pipeline catalysts, but after the March rerating and with shares near 52-week highs the setup looks more fully valued than asymmetric.

catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Leon Moulder Jr bought about $1.02M across March 30-31, 2026 and director Lu Hongbo added $1.50M on March 31 while short float sits at 30.58% with 8.02 days to cover after a roughly 46.2% YTD selloff, creating a credible squeeze setup with insider support.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Positive Phase 3 obexelimab data and meaningful insider buying support a recovery case, but the March 2026 financing overhang and unresolved commercialization debate keep Zenas in prove-it mode.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Ciena's March 5 beat-and-raise, combined with management's language about broad AI-driven networking demand, suggests optical transport is now participating in the same AI capex wave as switches and GPUs.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI-driven optical demand and raised FY2026 guidance support growth, but CIEN looks priced for perfection with rising cloud-customer concentration and no meaningful insider-buying signal.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Samsara's March 5 results showed AI features translating into enterprise spend, with ARR near $1.9 billion and record $1 million-plus ACV deals, which is the kind of monetized industrial-AI adoption most software names still lack.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Samsara is executing well with 30% ARR growth, positive free cash flow, and credible AI monetization, but the stock still screens premium and recent insider activity is mostly planned selling rather than fresh conviction buying.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Travere has a visible April 13, 2026 FDA decision for FILSPARI in FSGS, and approval would materially change the commercialization and valuation narrative for the company.
growth_picks.analyst_take: FILSPARI revenue is ramping and the April 13, 2026 FSGS FDA decision could unlock major upside, but the January review extension after a major amendment, unresolved efficacy debate, and recent insider selling keep the setup too binary for a BUY call.

Loar is a high-quality aerospace components compounder with rising guidance, insider buying, and meaningful short-interest fuel, but the stock still lacks a value cushion at roughly 54x forward earnings after debt-funded acquisitions.

Paychex is executing well after the Paycor deal and has fresh insider buying support, but acquisition debt, rate sensitivity, and muted near-term upside keep the risk/reward balanced rather than compelling.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Middle East fighting and Hormuz disruption tightened LNG balances in March 2026, and Cheniere's large flexible U.S. export platform is a direct beneficiary as Asian and European buyers pay up for non-Hormuz supply and sign longer contracts.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheniere remains a high-quality U.S. LNG exporter with visible 2026 cash flow and brownfield growth, but the stock is already near fair value and recent insider selling plus fresh debt issuance make the risk/reward unattractive for a new entry.

AI-driven semiconductor test demand is real and Teradyne's March product launches extend that edge, but the stock already reflects much of the upside at a rich multiple with limited analyst upside and no clear insider-buying signal.
Gartner looks optically cheap after the collapse, but I need proof at the May 2026 print that contract value and consulting are truly reaccelerating before upgrading it.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Replimune is a classic FDA-event setup with an April 10, 2026 action date for RP1 where approval could quickly re-rate a small-cap oncology story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Replimune has a real April 10, 2026 FDA catalyst and heavily shorted upside if RP1 is approved, but the prior Complete Response Letter, ongoing cash burn, and lack of recent open-market insider buying keep this as a binary event trade rather than a clean buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Hormuz transit risk has already driven tanker rates and insurance sharply higher in March 2026, and Frontline's large crude-tanker exposure gives it direct earnings leverage if disrupted Middle East flows keep rerouting cargoes into longer Atlantic basin voyages.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Frontline is executing well into a strong tanker market, but with the stock near its highs and the thesis tied to a volatile geopolitical rate spike, the setup looks supported rather than clearly mispriced.
PYPL is optically cheap and highly cash-generative, but branded-checkout deceleration, a weak 2026 guide, and a board-forced CEO reset mean the turnaround still needs proof before it merits a BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Lamb Weston is a real-business turnaround with a $5.87 billion market cap, positive net income, and a 36.99% drawdown from the 52-week high while trading just 6.72% above the low; the timing trigger is April 1, 2026 earnings plus Starboard's March 9 activist push for deeper cost cuts and strategic action, while the bear case is that frozen-potato demand and leverage stay too weak for activism to matter quickly.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Activist pressure, plant-rationalization self-help, and an executive-chair insider buy make Lamb Weston a credible turnaround candidate, but the operating recovery is still unproven because volume gains are being offset by pricing concessions and weak international profitability.
Cheap and cash-generative with an activist catalyst, but Snap still needs to prove that ad-product gains can outweigh user softness, dilution, and fierce ad competition.

FDA approval for acquired hypothalamic obesity and a positive EU CHMP opinion strengthen the commercial story, but the stock still carries high execution risk after the EMANATE Phase 3 miss and with reimbursement-driven uptake still unproven.

Nike has real balance-sheet and brand durability plus board-level insider buying, but the stock still looks too expensive for flat Q3 sales, tariff-driven margin pressure, and a China recovery that management says will take longer.

Defense-drone demand, backlog support, and recent contract/acquisition news keep the long-term story alive, but a March 2026 guidance cut, SCAR contract uncertainty, recent insider selling, and still-rich valuation keep AVAV below buy quality.
Stable cash-generative staples business, but the new Unilever Foods combination adds dilution, leverage, and execution risk that keeps MKC from being a clean contrarian buy here.

ADMA screens as cheap on forward earnings with real operating momentum and a live buyback, but the March 2026 channel-stuffing fight and receivables spike need Q1 cash-conversion proof before new buying.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Fiverr remains an avoid candidate because the AI tools that help higher-end freelancers also automate away low-end gigs, and the company's own February results still showed marketplace revenue declining even as management pivots toward an AI-native future.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Fiverr is statistically cheap, but shrinking buyers, declining core marketplace revenue, and negative 2026 guidance show the AI/upmarket pivot is still a turnaround story rather than a confirmed recovery.
Autonomy and energy optionality are real, but Tesla still screens as an overvalued momentum story while the core auto business is shrinking and insiders are selling rather than buying.

Spectrum-sale and restructuring catalysts are real, but EchoStar is already priced near its highs despite shrinking operations, going-concern language, heavy leverage, and unresolved regulatory execution risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Rocket Lab moved onto the radar after landing a record hypersonic-launch contract that strengthens the case for it as a scaled defense-space platform rather than only a small-launch story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Real defense-space momentum and launch execution are being overwhelmed by extreme valuation, Neutron timeline risk, and fresh equity dilution overhang.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Ziff Davis looks like an avoid candidate because after agreeing to sell its stable Connectivity unit, the remaining portfolio is more exposed to AI-answer and zero-click pressure in media categories such as health, shopping, and reviews.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The Accenture deal crystallizes value, but after that rerating Ziff Davis is left with a weaker, search-exposed media mix, deferred 2026 guidance, and no recent open-market insider buying to support a fresh long.
